title: Building Custom Workflows
description: Create your own analysis workflows by combining tools

steps.title: Workflow Overview
steps.description:
Workflows allow you to chain multiple tools together for automated analysis pipelines.

Define inputs, specify tool sequence, and configure parameters all in one place.
steps.tip: Workflows ensure reproducibility by capturing the complete analysis configuration.

steps.title: Step 1: Define Workflow Inputs
steps.description:
Specify the input data types your workflow will accept:

  • FASTQ files for raw sequence data
  • Reference genome for alignment
  • Parameter values for tool configuration
steps.onExecute: function(value) { return value ? 'Creating workflow: ' + value : 'Enter a workflow name'; }
steps.tip: Use descriptive names that indicate the workflow purpose and data type.

steps.title: Step 2: Add Tools to Workflow
steps.description:
Build your workflow by adding tools in execution order:

  1. Drag tools from the toolbox
  2. Connect them with data flow arrows
  3. Configure each tool's parameters
steps.onExecute: function(value) { return value ? 'Added ' + value + ' to workflow' : 'Select a tool'; }
steps.tip: Tools must be compatible - output format of one tool must match input format of the next.

steps.title: Step 3: Configure Tool Parameters
steps.description:
Set parameters for each tool in the workflow:

  • Quality thresholds
  • Reference paths
  • Output directories
  • Resource limits (threads, memory)
steps.tip: Use environment variables for paths that might change between runs.

steps.title: Step 4: Define Conditional Logic
steps.description:
Advanced workflows can include conditional branches:

  • Run different tools based on data characteristics
  • Split parallel processing for multiple samples
  • Merge results from parallel branches
steps.onExecute: function(value) { return value ? 'Condition set: ' + value : 'Condition optional'; }
steps.tip: Keep conditions simple to maintain workflow readability.

steps.title: Step 5: Define Outputs
steps.description:
Specify which intermediate and final results to save:

  • Final analysis results
  • Intermediate files for debugging
  • Summary statistics
  • Quality control reports
steps.tip: Use meaningful output names that describe the content.

steps.title: Step 6: Save and Validate
steps.description:
Save your workflow definition and validate it:

  • Check for missing dependencies
  • Verify parameter compatibility
  • Test with sample data
steps.onExecute: function() { return 'Workflow saved! Ready to execute with your data.'; }
steps.tip: Version control your workflows for tracking changes over time.

steps.title: Step 7: Execute Workflow
steps.description:
Run your workflow with input data. Monitor execution through the workflow dashboard.

Access results in the Data browser once complete.
steps.onExecute: function() { return 'Workflow execution started. Check status in Workflow Executions.'; }
steps.tip: Large workflows benefit from parallel execution of independent steps.

steps.title: Workflow Best Practices
steps.description:
Following these practices will help you build robust, reusable workflows:

  • Use meaningful names for workflow and outputs
  • Document parameter choices
  • Include quality control steps
  • Test incrementally as you build
  • Share workflows with collaborators
steps.tip: Export your workflow as JSON to share with team members or version control.
